differentials.name: Calcification > 1 cm in length obscures vessel lumen ◊  A jet associated with a stenosis of > 70% usually travels at least 1 cm downstream allowing conclusion about degree of obscured stenosis!
differentials.raw: Calcification > 1 cm in length obscures vessel lumen ◊  A jet associated with a stenosis of > 70% usually travels at least 1 cm downstream allowing conclusion about degree of obscured stenosis!

differentials.name: Contralateral high-grade stenosis =  ipsilateral ICA functions as collateral → increased blood flow velocities ◊  Use velocity ratios to compensate for this effect!
differentials.raw: Contralateral high-grade stenosis =  ipsilateral ICA functions as collateral → increased blood flow velocities ◊  Use velocity ratios to compensate for this effect!

differentials.name: SEVERE DISEASE = hemodynamically SIGNIFICANT LESION (a)  50-69% stenosis
differentials.raw: SEVERE DISEASE = hemodynamically SIGNIFICANT LESION (a)  50-69% stenosis

Calcification > 1 cm in length obscures: =  severity of stenosis is primarily graded as a ratio of lumen diameter narrowing NOT reduction in cross sectional area Limitations of assessment by US: Accuracy of duplex scans (compared to arteriography) for ICA lesions: 91-94% sensitivity, 85-99% specificity, 90-95% accuracy for > 50% ICA diameter stenosis
MINIMAL DISEASE: minimal spectral broadening in deceleration phase of systole

context: =  severity of stenosis is primarily graded as a ratio of lumen diameter narrowing NOT reduction in cross sectional area Limitations of assessment by US: Accuracy of duplex scans (compared to arteriography) for ICA lesions: 91-94% sensitivity, 85-99% specificity, 90-95% accuracy for > 50% ICA diameter stenosis √  no evidence of plaque √  peak systolic velocity (PSV) < 125 cm/sec √  no spectral broadening (= clear window under systole) √  minimal amount of plaque √  PSV < 125 cm/sec √  minimal spectral broadening in deceleration phase of systole √  moderate amount of plaque √  peak systole < 12
